description: Lock the professional patent examination and patent model collection nodes to their 1836/1840 U.S. Patent Office scope and prevent old Renaissance placeholder dependencies from returning.

checks.reason: The source-backed scope is the professional U.S. Patent Office examination system created by the Patent Act of 1836.
Professional patent examination is an administrative function inside a statutory patent-granting system.
The Patent Office needed filed application records, specifications, drawings, and models for examination.
Application drawings enabled technical inspection but are not the legal container for examination.
Printed patent publication is not the source-backed substrate for 1836 application examination.
The corrected node depends on the statutory patent-system category, not a broad invention-privilege predecessor.
The source-backed collection/display scope is the 1840 purpose-built Patent Office museum collection.
Submitted patent models had an application-examination purpose before public display.
The Patent Office retained and restored models, specifications, drawings, and records as a collection.
Public museum practice is display context, not a hard technical dependency.
The model collection was not merely a printed-specification publication system.
Printed cabinet catalogs are outside the corrected Patent Office model-gallery scope.
